subject: Statistics
title: Mixture Distributions
description: A fast breakdown of Mixture Distributions for builders.

hook.text: Sometimes data has two peaks. The average sits in a valley where almost no data points exist.

buildup.text: A mixture distribution combines two or more distributions. Each peak represents a different group.

discovery.text: Human heights look normal — until you realize it's a mixture of male and female distributions.

twist.text: Ignoring mixtures leads to nonsense statistics. The average of two groups can describe neither.

climax.text: Identifying hidden subgroups is one of the most valuable things a statistician can do.

punchline.text: One average for two groups describes neither.

quiz.question: What is a mixture distribution?
options: A combination of two or more distributions from distinct subgroups
A distribution that mixes qualitative and quantitative data
Any distribution with more than one peak
